folio.label: 149v
folio.l: And thow shalt kisse , the relykes euäychon
ña for a grote , vn bokle a noon thy purs
Nay nay q¶ he , tha~ne haue I Cristes curs
Lat be quod he , it shal nat be so thiche
Thow woldest make me kisse , thyne olde breche
And swere it were a Relyke , of a seynt
Thougú it were witú thy foundement , depeynt
But by the Croys , whicú that Seynt Elyne fonde
I wolde I hadde , thyne coylouns in myn honde
In stede of relikes , or of Seyntuarie
Lat cut hem of , I wil the helpe hem carie
They shuln be Shryned , in an hogges tord
This Pardoner answered , nat a word
So wrotú he was , no word ne wold he seye
Now quod oure hoost , I wol no lengere pleye
Witú the , ne witú noon other angry man
But rigút a noon , the worthy knygút bygan
Whan that he saw , that al the peeple low
Nomore of this , for it is rigút I now
Sire Pardonere be glad , and mery of chere
And ©e Sire hoost , that ben to me so dere
I preye ©ow that ©e kisse , the Pardonere
And îdonere I preye the , drawe the nere
And as we diden , lat vs laugúe and pleye
Anoon they kisten , & reden fortú here weye
